data.title: Economist Peter Schiff explains why Bitcoin can't replace the dollar, cites "greater fool" theory. Central banks cannot hold Bitcoin as reserves. Gold has outperformed S&P this century.
data.description: On Monday, economist Peter Schiff discussed Bitcoin's potential as a global reserve currency on the Tucker Carlson show. He highlighted that despite the declining value of the dollar, Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) would not replace it due to its dependency on the "greater fool" theory. This theory suggests that people invest in assets not based on intrinsic value but with the anticipation of selling them at a higher price to a "greater fool" later on. Schiff emphasized that central banks cannot hold Bitcoin as reserves, as selling it would lead to a sharp price drop. He argued that tangible assets like gold are essential for reserve holdings. Schiff also noted that gold has significantly outperformed the S&P this century. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: $2.2B outflows from top stablecoins hint at capital exit amid flat Bitcoin trading, investors opting for fiat over dip.
data.description: In the span of just 10 days, leading stablecoins have experienced a collective decrease in value amounting to $2.2 billion. This significant drop suggests that investors are opting to convert their holdings to fiat currency instead of holding onto them in anticipation of a market rebound.

data.title: Elliott Management advises against Toyota Industries Corp's Revised TOB terms, urges shareholders not to tender.
data.description: Elliott Investment Management L.P. and Elliott Advisors (UK) Limited, collectively known as Elliott, announced on January 26, 2026, that they do not plan to submit their shares to the Revised Tender Offer to Buy (TOB) at the existing terms. Elliott advises funds and strongly advises other shareholders against tendering their shares.

data.title: Envision's green hydrogen and ammonia project in Inner Mongolia recognized by World Economic Forum as global benchmark.
data.description: A groundbreaking green hydrogen and ammonia project in Chifeng, Inner Mongolia by Envision has been highlighted in the World Economic Forum's latest white paper. This project is recognized as a global case study. Located in Chifeng, Inner Mongolia, the initiative showcases innovative sustainable practices. The World Economic Forum's publication titled "From Blueprint to Reality: A Stronger Business" focuses on this specific project. Envision's efforts in sustainable energy production have gained international attention, indicating a shift towards cleaner energy sources. This recognition underscores the importance of renewable energy projects in addressing environmental challenges. The project's impact extends globally, emphasizing the significance of embracing eco-friendly solutions. The World Economic Forum's choice to feature this project demonstrates its significance in the transition towards sustainable development.

data.title: Chainlink Labs partners with WEMADE-Led KRW Stablecoin Alliance 'GAKS' to boost Korean digital asset standards, providing technical support and leveraging Chainlink platform.
data.description: Chainlink Labs is set to offer valuable assistance in establishing technical standards and enhancing infrastructure using their extensive global market knowledge. They will also provide opportunities for alliance members to make use of the Chainlink platform for various tokenized asset use cases. This update comes after a recent collaboration with the alliance, which includes over 100 organizations and boasts a combined market capitalization exceeding $5 billion. The partnership aims to further innovate and expand the capabilities of tokenized assets through Chainlink's support and expertise.

data.title: Mitsubishi Electric invests in Lucend, AI startup for data center ops optimization. ME Fund's 14th investment.
data.description: In Tokyo, Mitsubishi Electric Corporation (Stock Ticker: 6503) revealed the latest investment by its ME Innovation Fund in Lucend, a U.S. startup specializing in AI technology for enhancing data center operations. This marks the fund’s fourteenth investment so far. The surge in digitalization, particularly the growing utilization of generative AI, has spurred increased capital injections into data centers globally. Simultaneously, data center operators are facing rising demands for optimization.

data.title: Mitsubishi Electric discovers self-recovery property of HOPG with Kyoto University, extending MEMS lifespan. (count: 96)
data.description: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation (Stock Symbol: 6503) has revealed the groundbreaking discovery of the world's initial self-repairing characteristic of highly oriented pyrolytic graphite (HOPG), a van der Waals (vdW)-layered substance. This development, made in collaboration with the Solid Mechanics Laboratory (Hirakata Laboratory) of Kyoto University's Graduate School of Engineering, is anticipated to enhance the lifespan of micro electro mechanical systems (MEMS) thanks to the use of vdW-layered materials.
Micron is the first to announce a NAND capacity increase, excluding Kioxia and YMTC.

data.title: XTransfer expands in Latin America at INTERMODA 2026, showcasing at the International Apparel Exhibition in Mexico, January 20-23.
data.description: XTransfer, a prominent B2B cross-border trade payment platform, is making a significant move by featuring at INTERMODA 2026 in Mexico, the International Apparel and Textile Fabrics Exhibition. The event, taking place from January 20 to 23, signifies a crucial step in XTransfer's goal to connect worldwide traders with the thriving Latin American market. INTERMODA, known as the biggest fashion trade event in the region, boasting over 40 years of successful history, will serve as a strategic platform for XTransfer to showcase its offerings and expand its presence.
KOSPI gains 2% as Samsung Electronics rallies 3% in Seoul trading.

data.title: Twin Hospitality Group (Nasdaq: TWNP) files for voluntary Chapter 11 to strengthen capital structure, support growth.
data.description: A company called Twin Hospitality Group Inc. (Nasdaq: TWNP) which owns Twin Peaks Restaurant has recently initiated voluntary chapter 11 proceedings in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas. This move is aimed at reducing debt, optimizing value for shareholders, and aiding in the expansion of their brand.

data.title: FAT Brands Inc. files voluntary Chapter 11, seeks to deleverage balance sheet for growth. (Words: 20)
data.description: On January 26, 2026, FAT Brands Inc. (NASDAQ: FAT) revealed its initiation of voluntary chapter 11 proceedings in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas. The company aims to utilize these filings to reduce its debt, enhance value for stakeholders, and foster the ongoing expansion of its brands.
